mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in real retail sales signals robust consumer demand, lifting corporate revenue expectations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are not positive in more than 50% of months when RSAFS is trending higher, or the Rank IC between RSAFS changes and forward returns falls below 0.03 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate of more than 0.3 percentage points over a three-month window signals labor market deterioration that reduces household income growth and corporate earnings forecasts, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in greater than 55% of triggered instances, or the mean return conditional on the trigger is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: The interaction of rising retail sales and payroll employment signals that consumer spending is funded by labor income rather than credit, creating a sustainable demand impulse that generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next month; the joint effect is stronger than either series in isolation.
failure_condition: The interaction term between RSAFS and PAYEMS produces a Rank IC below 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window, or a model with the interaction does not significantly outperform an additive specification in predicting positive returns.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during early-cycle recovery periods signals rising capacity utilization and operating leverage for cyclical firms, leading to positive short-term S&P 500 returns over the next week as margins expand.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration coincides with negative 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of identified early-cycle periods over a 5-year walk-forward window.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate sustained below 1% reflects deep monetary accommodation that suppresses risk-free rates and pushes investors out the risk curve, generating positive quarterly S&P 500 returns through valuation expansion.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in the majority of months when FEDFUNDS is below 1%, or the Sharpe ratio conditional on the trigger is not statistically greater than zero.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls signals rising aggregate wage income and durable consumption capacity, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent week as equity investors revise growth expectations upward.
failure_condition: Positive PAYEMS growth months fail to deliver positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of observations, or the Rank IC between PAYEMS changes and forward returns falls below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts sustained above 1.2 million units signal durable construction demand and broad multiplier effects through building materials, durable goods, and employment, lifting cyclical equity exposures and generating positive monthly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day returns during periods when HOUST exceeds 1200 are not significantly higher than during periods when HOUST is at or below 1200 (t-test p > 0.10), or positive returns occur in fewer than 55% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: CPI inflation accelerating above a 3% annualized threshold erodes real consumer purchasing power and raises expectations of tighter monetary policy, leading to negative quarterly S&P 500 returns as real discount rates reprice higher.
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 60-day returns in greater than 50% of instances when the CPIAUCSL trigger is active, or the mean forward return conditional on the trigger is not significantly negative.
